The '''''Lulsla''''' was a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[MC40a light cruiser/Legends|MC40a Light Calamari Cruiser]].

==History==
The vessel performed an important role in the Rebel Alliance leadership's flight from [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]], by dispatching starfighters to capture [[Outpost D-34]], an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] checkpoint located along a Rebel escape route.

After ''Lulsla'' was positively identified as the source of the forces that attacked and plundered the outpost, Imperials planned a counterattack to intercept it while under supply in the [[Bruanii sector/Legends|Bruanii sector]]. While standard Imperial doctrine would have meant sending [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]] to engage it in ship-to-ship combat, the Rebels had preempted this by laying a vast minefield of [[Mine Type A|Type A]] and [[Mine Type C|Type C mines]].

From the ''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer [[Hammer (Imperial-class)|''Hammer'']] and the [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|Modified Frigate]] ''[[Fogger]]'', two hyperspace attacks were launched against the ''Lulsla''.

===Disrupt resupply===
During the first attack, [[Delta-class DX-9 stormtrooper transport|''Delta''-class stormtrooper transports]] ''[[Sigma group]]'' and ''[[Omega]]'' were tasked with destroying the resupply containers with [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedoes]] from standoff distance outside the minefield. Fighter escort was provided by two squadrons of [[Alpha-class Xg-1 Star Wing/Legends|Assault Gunboats]], armed with [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missiles]].

As one of the Rebellion's front-line cruisers, ''Lulsla'' had a full complement of modern starfighters, including [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wings]], [[RZ-1 A-wing interceptor/Legends|A-wings]], and [[A/SF-01 B-wing starfighter/Legends|B-wings]]. The Gunboats led by [[Maarek Stele#Defense of Outpost D-34|Maarek Stele]] largely eliminated the Mon Calamari Rebel cruiser's fighter screen and a significant number of mines, though his wingmen suffered heavy losses at the hands of A-wing and X-wing reinforcements.

With the first wave having successfully accomplished its goals, the surviving attackers escaped into hyperspace, though Stele inspected several containers and realized that they stored Imperial equipment, which he later confided to the [[Prophets of the Dark Side]]. (It was later discovered that Admiral [[Harkov]] had been selling it to the Rebellion.)

===Destruction of ''Lulsla''===
The Imperials wasted no time conducting a follow-up assault. Transports ''Sigma'' and ''Omega'' were rearmed with [[Proton rocket/Legends|heavy rockets]], and again would be accompanied by escorting Assault Gunboats.

This caught ''Lulsla'' at its most vulnerable point, as it was trapped by its own minefield and reinforcements could not arrive in time. A handful of X-wings and Y-wings put up some resistance, but to no avail. The Imperials accomplished their objective with little loss.

The officers aboard the ''Lulsla'' abandoned their doomed ship and attempted to flee. However, their shuttles were quickly caught and disabled by Stele and captured by stormtrooper transports.

==Appearances==
*[[Star Wars: TIE Fighter (video game)|''Star Wars: TIE Fighter'']] {{1st}}
